intro:
There is not enough information available on Atelophragma strigulosum, and it is not recognized as a valid species by most botanical databases.

However, I can provide information on Atelophragma, which is a genus of flowering plants in the family Aristolochiaceae. 

Atelophragma is a genus of flowering plants, commonly known as Dutchman's pipe,  that is native to Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Atelophragma species include aristolochic acids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. 

Due to the potential toxicity of aristolochic acids, Atelophragma is not commonly used in herbal preparations and is not recommended for internal use.
